channel.title: NPR Topics: Music
channel.description: Tiny Desks, All Songs Considered and in-depth features from NPR
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Tomeka Reid expands the possibilities of jazz avant garde on 'Dance! Skip! Hop!'
item.description: Reid's quartet makes music that seems inspired by a variety of sources, ranging from modern dance to children's games. Her latest album is one of her most compelling.
item.content:encoded: Reid's quartet makes music that seems inspired by a variety of sources, ranging from modern dance to children's games. Her latest album is one of her most compelling.
item.dc:creator: Martin Johnson

item.title: Rostam quilts Americana with Persian influences on 'American Stories'
item.description: Rostam explains why he's embracing his Iranian roots on his new album, American Stories.
item.content:encoded:
Rostam explains why he's embracing his Iranian roots on his new album, American Stories.

(Image credit: Anthony Muse)
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: 'SNL' just wrapped its 51st season: It's time to cruelly rank its musical guests
item.description: The latest season of Saturday Night Live felt transitional in many ways, but it was full of blockbuster pop stars, up-and-coming bands and musical legends. We ranked them from worst to best.
item.content:encoded:
The latest season of Saturday Night Live felt transitional in many ways, but it was full of blockbuster pop stars, up-and-coming bands and musical legends. We ranked them from worst to best.

(Image credit: Will Heath)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Thompson

item.title: Drake, in rebuilding mode, goes all in by releasing three albums at once
item.description: With his first major solo output since his battle with Kendrick Lamar in 2024 ended, Drake does what Drake has always done: puts out more music than most people could reasonably consume.
item.content:encoded: With his first major solo output since his battle with Kendrick Lamar in 2024 ended, Drake does what Drake has always done: puts out more music than most people could reasonably consume.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Carmichael

item.title: Through the American centuries, 'Amazing Grace' endures
item.description: The song, penned before American Independence by a slave trader turned abolitionist, has remained a source of comfort, inspiration and redemption.
item.content:encoded:
The song, penned before American Independence by a slave trader turned abolitionist, has remained a source of comfort, inspiration and redemption.

(Image credit: Eric Piper)
item.dc:creator: Lara Downes

item.title: Pop star Shakira is acquitted in a Spanish tax fraud case
item.description: The decision follows years of tax troubles in Spain for the Colombian superstar. Spanish tax authorities did not prove that the singer was a resident of Spain, the court said in its decision.
item.content:encoded:
The decision follows years of tax troubles in Spain for the Colombian superstar. Spanish tax authorities did not prove that the singer was a resident of Spain, the court said in its decision.

(Image credit: Charles Sykes)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Vince Gill: Tiny Desk Concert
item.description: In power ballads and two-steppers, Vince Gill crafts intimate songs about how we treat each other. The Country Music Hall of Famer sandwiches '90s hits in between new material, and closes with "Go Rest High on That Mountain."
item.content:encoded: In power ballads and two-steppers, Vince Gill crafts intimate songs about how we treat each other. The Country Music Hall of Famer sandwiches '90s hits in between new material, and closes with "Go Rest High on That Mountain."
item.dc:creator: Lars Gotrich

item.title: Isaiah Rashad: 'Time's up for being afraid of yourself'
item.description: In the five years since his last album, the Chattanooga rapper retreated from the public eye after a very private betrayal. He emerges with raw new music in pursuit of a warts-and-all self-acceptance.
item.content:encoded:
In the five years since his last album, the Chattanooga rapper retreated from the public eye after a very private betrayal. He emerges with raw new music in pursuit of a warts-and-all self-acceptance.

(Image credit: Quil Lemons)
item.dc:creator: Rodney Carmichael

item.title: The Eurovision Song Contest reaches its grand final with pop and protests
item.description: The final of the Eurovision Contest arrives Saturday, with tight security and rainy weather failing to dent the enthusiasm of fans, or the opposition of critics who think Israel shouldn't be invited.
item.content:encoded:
The final of the Eurovision Contest arrives Saturday, with tight security and rainy weather failing to dent the enthusiasm of fans, or the opposition of critics who think Israel shouldn't be invited.

(Image credit: Martin Meissner)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 'It feels like it's the end of the world to me,' says musician Dua Saleh
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with the musician Dua Saleh about how they channeled the trauma and grief of their childhood in East Africa into music, for their new album Of Earth & Wires.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with the musician Dua Saleh about how they channeled the trauma and grief of their childhood in East Africa into music, for their new album Of Earth & Wires.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink
